_note: Per-book source-coverage matrix (Spec 383 §1). Read before writing a finding's Iron-Law Source so a book is cited ONLY when the observed symptom matches its principle — prevents shallow book-name-citation. Book IDs match decay-risks.json sources (one book registry, two consumers). The book COUNT is derived from this file (rule 8) — adding a book needs no code edit.

do_not_ignore: A single coherent design owner is not 'too few cooks' — conceptual integrity outranks parallelism

encoded: Ch. 11: The Power of Variable Names
do_not_ignore: A long routine that is a single cohesive sequence is not automatically a smell — measure responsibilities, not lines alone
A smell is a hint to investigate, not a verdict; a justified deviation with a comment is not debt
A composition root legitimately wires concrete dependencies — it is not DIP debt

encoded: Ch. 5: Decoupling and the Law of Demeter
do_not_ignore: Coincidental duplication (two things that look alike but change for different reasons) is not a DRY violation
A CRUD transaction-script is a legitimate choice for a simple subdomain — not every workflow needs a rich domain model
A thin module that is a genuine adapter/boundary is not automatically shallow-by-smell
Not every observable behaviour is a contract you must freeze — weigh the dependency surface
Multiple assertions on ONE logical concept are not Assertion Roulette — count concepts, not assert calls
An integration test that legitimately touches a real collaborator is not a 'not isolated' unit-test failure

encoded: Ch. 1: Legacy code is code without tests
do_not_ignore: Untested code that is about to be deleted is not worth a characterization test — weigh the change vector
The 70:20:10 ratio is a guideline, not a quota — a domain with heavy integration risk legitimately skews
